Introduction.student_text:
An animal cell is the basic unit of life in animals. It is eukaryotic, meaning
it has a nucleus. It does not have a cell wall like plant cells.
Introduction.model_text:
An animal cell is the basic structural and functional unit of all animal organisms.
It is a eukaryotic cell it has a membrane-bound nucleus and membrane-bound organelles.
Animal cells lack a cell wall, chloroplasts, and a large central vacuole.

Labels: Cell Membrane, Nucleus, Nucleolus, Mitochondria,
Endoplasmic Reticulum, Golgi Body, Ribosome, Centriole.

Cell_Membrane.student_text:
The cell membrane surrounds the cell and controls what enters and leaves it.
It is made of a phospholipid bilayer and is selectively permeable.
Cell_Membrane.model_text:
The cell membrane (plasma membrane) is a flexible phospholipid bilayer that
surrounds the cell. It controls the movement of substances in and out of the
cell (selective permeability). It also maintains cell shape and enables
cell-to-cell communication via surface receptors.

Nucleus.student_text:
The nucleus is the control centre of the cell. It contains DNA and chromosomes.
It is surrounded by the nuclear envelope.
(Did not mention nucleolus or nuclear pores)
Nucleus.model_text:
The nucleus is the control centre of the cell. It is enclosed by the nuclear
envelope (double membrane with nuclear pores). It contains DNA in the form of
chromosomes, which carry genetic information. The nucleolus inside the nucleus
is responsible for producing ribosomes.

Cytoplasm.student_text:
Cytoplasm is the fluid inside the cell. It contains the organelles.
Mitochondria produce energy (ATP). Ribosomes make proteins.
(No mention of ER, Golgi Body, or Centriole)
Cytoplasm.model_text:
Cytoplasm is the jelly-like fluid (cytosol) that fills the cell between the
cell membrane and the nucleus. It contains all organelles:
Mitochondria: powerhouse of the cell; produces ATP via cellular respiration.
Endoplasmic Reticulum (ER): rough ER has ribosomes for protein synthesis;
smooth ER is involved in lipid synthesis and detoxification.
Golgi Body: packages and ships proteins and lipids to their destination.
Ribosome: site of protein synthesis.
Centriole: involved in cell division (forms spindle fibres).

Conclusion.student_text:
The animal cell has many organelles that work together to keep the cell alive.
Each part has its own function.
Conclusion.model_text:
The animal cell is a highly organised unit. Each organelle performs a specific
function vital to the survival and proper functioning of the organism.
Together, they carry out life processes such as energy production, protein
synthesis, waste removal, and reproduction, making the cell the fundamental
unit of life.
